settings.title: Settings
settings.ha.location_tooltip: Periodically reports this device’s location via the Mobile App integration
settings.appearance.language_restart_not: Restart required to apply language change
settings.appearance.dimming_tooltip: Fade button color based on brightness level
settings.appearance.glass_tooltip: Use a translucent glass background for the window
settings.appearance.pin_tooltip: Keep the window open when clicking outside it
settings.shortcut.label: App Toggle:
settings.shortcut.kde_hint: On KDE Wayland, the app-toggle shortcut is managed by KDE. Change it in KDE Shortcuts instead of recording it here.
settings.shortcut.wayland_hint: Global app-toggle shortcuts are not currently supported on this Wayland desktop. Create a custom keybind that runs ‘prism-desktop --toggle’ instead. The in-window entity shortcuts still work while Prism is focused.
button_editor.show_ids_tooltip: Switch between showing friendly names and entity IDs globally
button_editor.display_style_tooltip: How the sensor value is rendered
button_editor.sun_timer_tooltip: When enabled and button is 2+ wide, shows time until next sunrise or sunset
button_editor.album_art_tooltip: Display album artwork as button background
button_editor.animated_bg_tooltip: Enable animated parallax background when no album art is shown
button_editor.automation_tooltip: Toggle enables/disables the automation. Trigger runs it immediately.
button_editor.lock_tooltip: Toggle logic: If locked -> Unlock, If unlocked -> Lock.
notifications.glass_ui_warning: Glass UI is experimental — not optimised for light mode and may impact performance on low-end hardware.
notifications.page_full: Page {page_num} is full. Cannot move button.
notifications.move_as_1x1: No room at {orig_w}×{orig_h} on Page {page_num}. Move as 1×1?
notifications.update_available: Prism Desktop {version} is available. Download now?
notifications.missing_credentials: Missing URL or token — fill in both fields first.
notifications.language_restart: Language changed — please restart Prism Desktop to apply.
welcome.step_1.title: Welcome to Prism Desktop
welcome.step_1.body: Your Home Assistant control panel lives in the system tray. Click the tray icon — or press Ctrl+Alt+H — to show or hide this panel. You can change the shortcut anytime in Settings.
welcome.step_2.title: Moving & Resizing
welcome.step_2.body: Drag the background to reposition the panel anywhere on your screen. Drag the left edge to change the number of columns, or the top/bottom edge to change rows.
welcome.step_3.title: Multiple Pages
welcome.step_3.body: Scroll the mouse wheel over the panel to switch pages. The dots at the bottom show which page you’re on — you can add more pages in Settings → Appearance.
welcome.step_4.title: Connecting to Home Assistant
welcome.step_4.body: Open Settings (tray icon → Settings) and enter your Home Assistant URL and a Long-Lived Access Token. Generate one in your HA profile under Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens → Create Token.
welcome.step_5.title: You’re All Set
welcome.step_5.body: Press + on any empty slot to add your first entity. Right-click any button to edit, duplicate, or remove it. Enjoy Prism Desktop!
